name: Livegrep
description: Livegrep — Fast, real-time regex code search engine for large monorepos with browser-based navigation.

seo.title: Livegrep — Code Assistants / Agent Tooling
seo.meta_description: Livegrep: Livegrep — Fast, real-time regex code search engine for large monorepos with browser-based navigation.

when_to_use: Running sub-second regex search across multi-million-line codebases
Building internal code search tools for large engineering orgs
Replacing slow git grep with real-time indexed code search
Indexing and searching cross-repository code patterns instantly

pros: Sub-second regex search across massive codebases
Browser UI responsive enough for interactive exploration
Open source with proven use at Stripe and others
No AI dependency, just reliable regex matching
cons: Self-hosting and indexing setup is nontrivial
Regex-only means no semantic or AST-level understanding
UI feels dated compared to Sourcegraph
Project activity has slowed in recent years
review.verdict: Still one of the fastest code search tools for regex, but Sourcegraph or Zoekt offer more modern options.
review.best_for: Organizations with huge monorepos needing fast regex search without SaaS fees
review.not_for: Teams wanting semantic search, AI-powered navigation, or low-ops managed tooling

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free / hobby tier; no peak-lock evidence.
